patient_profile.diagnosis: Stable angina with positive stress test (I20.9)

denial_pattern_sources: unreasonable_documentation_deadline: DOL ERISA Claims Procedure Regulations 29 CFR § 2560.503-1(g); AMA 2023 Prior Authorization Survey
algo_boilerplate_fingerprint: JAMA Health Forum Feb 2024 (AI in coverage decisions); ProPublica 'Uncovered' Series 2023
denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

This letter advises you of our determination regarding your provider's request on 09/07/2026 for Coronary CT angiography related to Stable angina with positive stress test (I20.9). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied Cigna Medical Coverage Policies and Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG). The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. A lower level of care is appropriate for Stable angina with positive stress test (I20.9); Coronary CT angiography exceeds what is required. Outpatient management has not been exhausted per clinical guidelines.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with level of care too high. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

Cigna Health Care Management
clinical_context: The 27-year-old member has Stable angina with positive stress test (I20.9). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Coronary CT angiography recommended as the next appropriate step. Typical angina class II; stress ECG with 2 mm ST depression at 7 METs. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that level of care too high bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if his care is delayed further.
